prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='inverse_rmse_weights'. Narrative: THETA leads trend tracking (slope error 0.9682) while ARIMA excels at seasonality capture (correlation 0.4809). ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall with lowest RMSE (15517.9), but high seasonality variance (0.7589) indicates models capture cyclic patterns very differently. Moderate RMSE spread ratio (0.269) suggests inverse RMSE weighting is appropriate, leveraging tier1 models while accounting for seasonal fit differences.

raw.pattern_analyst:

  "tier2_models": ["DWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "rf", "ETS", "THETA", "FT_rf", "CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "inverse_rmse_weights",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "seasonality",
  "key_insights": {

    "high_disagreement": false,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]

  "cot_narrative": "THETA leads trend tracking (slope error 0.9682) while ARIMA excels at seasonality capture (correlation 0.4809). ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall with lowest RMSE (15517.9), but high seasonality variance (0.7589) indicates models capture cyclic patterns very differently. Moderate RMSE spread ratio (0.269) suggests inverse RMSE weighting is appropriate, leveraging tier1 models while accounting for seasonal fit differences."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: THETA leads trend tracking (slope error 0.9682) while ARIMA excels at seasonality capture (correlation 0.4809). ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall with lowest RMSE (15517.9), but high seasonality variance (0.7589) indicates models capture cyclic patterns very differently. Moderate RMSE spread ratio (0.269) suggests inverse RMSE weighting is appropriate, leveraging tier1 models while accounting for seasonal fit differences.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high heterogeneity across horizons, favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.269 (≥0.25) suggests robust combination approaches like dba_combination are appropriate. RMSE spread ratio=0.269 is moderate, supporting inverse_rmse_weights over full mean. n_windows=3 ≤ 4 justifies small top_k=3-4 with shrinkage=0.25 to prevent overfitting. pattern_analyst recommends 'inverse_rmse_weights' as the method hint.
